observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Management agreed on the: a. Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report to be submitted to COA; and b. General Service Office to prepare and maintain Property Cards and reconcile its records with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing and the actual physical counting by the Management was not yet completed.
observations.reason: It was observed that the physical counting conducted by the Management was not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: Provide the Audit Team with the Municipal Ordinances that established the LEEs; Stop the practice of subsidizing the operations of the LEEs; and Re-evaluate the performance of these LEEs in as much as self-sustainability is concerned, otherwise, consider the options suggested under Paragraph No. 3.4 of LBC 100.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by the Management.
observations.reason: Subsidy to LEE still exists for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: Evaluate the manpower requirement of the treasurer’s office; Reorient the concerned personnel of the possible procedures, administrative or judicial, and introduce improvements in the process to improve collection efficiency on delinquent taxes.
observations.managementAction: The collection remedies were not fully exhausted; collection case still exists and actions to improve collection efficiency to be undertaken.
observations.reason: Collection Case still exists in CY 2024. To be reiterated in the current Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Disposal
Committee gather the related disposal documents and submit the
same to the Office of the Municipal Accountant to serve as a basis for the
derecognition of the disposed assets.

1.8. The Accounting Office, upon the receipt of the supporting documents
needed, to take up the necessary adjusting entries to the correct
balances of the PPE sub - account and its corresponding contra asset
account.

1.9. Henceforth, strengthen asset management by implementing regular
monitoring and enhancing coordination among the Disposal
Committee, GSO, and Accounting offices.

We recommended, and the Management agreed for the: a) Drop from the books of Local Government Unit (LGU) of the transferred properties to the barangays; and b) Prepare necessary adjusting entries for the undelivered properties.
observations.managementAction: An SB Resolution was made to transfer the accounts to the barangays; an adjusting entry was made to drop the accounts per JEV Nos. 2023-06-003117, 003118, and 003119.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Management agreed for the: a) Municipal Treasury Office and Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile their records against the RPTODL; and b) Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct RPT and SET Receivable balances.
observations.managementAction: The accounts have been reconciled by the Treasury and the Municipal Accounting Office and adjusting entries were prepared to reflect the correct balances (JEV Nos. 2023-02-000020 and 2023-02-000586).
observations.reason: Adjustments were made by the Accounting office to reconcile the balances between the two accounting offices concern.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Management agreed that: The Municipal Treasurer closely monitors all collecting officers and ensure that all collections are remitted within the prescribed period, otherwise issue a demand letter and impose sanctions when necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer closely monitors all collecting officers and ensure that all collections are remitted within the prescribed period; the Municipal Treasurer require the claimants to fill up the Box E of the Disbursement Vouchers; the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Accounting Office the Monthly RCI together with the schedule of unreleased checks; the Municipal Accountant record the transaction only upon the receipt of the RCI from the Treasury Office.
observations.reason: The audit observation was adhered to by the Management, thereby resolving the said audit findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Management agreed to: (a) exhaust all means to ensure the immediate and complete delivery of the procured items; (b) impose liquidated damages to the supplier for the undue delays incurred; and (c) henceforth refrain from paying in advance the procured items without complete delivery.
observations.managementAction: A demand was given to the supplier through the Veterinary Office and liquidated damages was imposed and paid by the supplier. All items were already delivered.
observations.reason: Items are already delivered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Management agreed to: (a) exhaust all means to ensure the immediate and complete delivery of the procured items; (b) impose liquidated damages to the supplier for the undue delays incurred; and (c) henceforth refrain from paying in advance the procured items without delivery.
observations.managementAction: A demand was given to the supplier through the LDRRM Office. Liquidated damages was imposed and paid by the supplier. Items are already delivered and used.
observations.reason: Items are already delivered and used.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the management agreed that: The Municipal Treasurer require the claimants to fill up Box E of the Disbursement Vouchers; The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Accounting Office the Monthly RCI together with the schedule of unreleased checks; The Municipal Accountant record the transaction only upon the receipt of the RCI from the Treasury Office.
observations.managementAction: Proper coordination and management of both offices were established. The Municipal Treasurer strictly require the claimants to fill up the Box E of the Disbursement Vouchers; the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Accounting Office the Monthly RCI together with the schedule of unreleased checks; the Municipal Accountant record the transaction only upon the receipt of the RCI from the Treasury Office.
observations.reason: The audit observation was adhered to by the Management, thereby resolving the said audit findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Management agreed to correct the erroneous entries by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and crediting Construction-in-Progress.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment were already made by the Municipal Accountant to correct the erroneous entries by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and crediting Construction-in-Progress.
observations.reason: The audit observation was adhered to by the Management, thereby resolving the said audit findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: Require the MDRRM Officer to prepare annually a comprehensive LDRRMFIP that includes, under separate caption, list of projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of the previous years’ LDRRMF; and Consider the utilization of the unexpended balance of the previous years’ LDRRMF in line with the guidelines set forth in RA 10121 or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Officer was required to prepare annually a comprehensive LDRRMFIP that includes the list of projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of the previous years' LDRRMF; consideration was given to the utilization of the unexpended balance in line with the guidelines set forth in RA 10121.
observations.reason: The audit observation was adhered by the Management, thereby resolving the said audit findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to: Implement projects in the first semester of CY 2024 and if found to be incapable of implementing as such, to return the funds to the source agency immediately in the succeeding semester.
observations.managementAction: Five out of six programs were implemented in the first semester of CY 2024. If found to be incapable of implementing as such, return the funds to the source agency in the immediate succeeding semester.
observations.reason: Five out of six programs were already implemented in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed for the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report to be submitted to COA; and 



b. General Service Office to prepare and maintain Property Cards and reconcile its records with the accounting records,
observations.managementAction: Ongoing actual physical counting by the Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: It was observed that the physical count conducted by the Management was not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to correct the erroneous entries by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and crediting Construction-in-Progress.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Provide the Audit Team with the Municipal Ordinances that created the LEEs;
Stop the practice of subsidizing the operations of the LEEs; and
Re-evaluate the performance of these LEEs in as much as                       self-sustainability is concerned, otherwise, consider the options suggested under Paragraph No. 3.4 of LBC 100.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Require the MDRRM Officer to prepare annually a comprehensive LDRRMFIP that includes, under separate caption, list of projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of previous years’ LDRRMF; and
Consider the utilization of the unexpended balance of the previous years’ LDRRMF in line with the guidelines set forth in RA 10121 or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010.

We recommended and the management agreed to implement projects in the first semester of CY 2024 and if found to be incapable of implementing such, to return the funds to the source agency in the immediately succeeding semester.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Institute administrative and/or judicial action against delinquent taxpayers to avoid prescription of the right of the Municipality to collect such taxes:
Evaluate the manpower requirement of the treasurer’s office; and
Reorient the concerned personnel of the possible procedures, administrative or judicial, and introduce improvements in the process to improve collection efficiency on delinquent taxes.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to:

Drop from the books of accounts the transferred properties to barangays; and

Prepare necessary adjusting entries for the undelivered disaster equipment.
observations.managementAction: SB Resolution has been made to transfer the property without cause. Adjusting entry was made to drop the accounts to the books per JEV Nos. 2023-06-003117, 003118 and 003119.
observations.reason: The audit observation was adhered by the Management thereby resolving the said audit findings.

Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct RPT and SET Receivable balances.

observations.reason: Adjustments were already made by the Accounting office to reconcile the balances between the two offices concern.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitors all collecting officers and ensure that all collections are remitted within the prescribed period, otherwise issue a demand letter and impose sanctions when necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal treasurer with due diligence is monitoring the collecting officers of the Management, making reasonable assurance that all collections will be remitted within the prescribed period and in accordance with the guidelines.
observations.reason: The Management already addressed the audit findings. However, continuous monitoring by the Audit Team is needed to ensure that the said issue will no longer recur.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: (a) exhaust all means to ensure the immediate and complete delivery of the procured items, (b) impose liquidated damages to the supplier for the undue delays incurred, and (c) henceforth refrain from paying in advance the procured items without complete delivery.
observations.managementAction: A demand was given to the supplier through the Veterinary Office and liquidated damages was imposed and paid by the supplier. All items were already delivered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: (a) exhaust all means to ensure the immediate and complete delivery of the procured items, (b) impose liquidated damages to the supplier for the undue delays incurred, and (c) henceforth refrain from paying in advance the procured items without delivery.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the GSO to maintain updated Stock Cards of inventories
and to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records with the
Ledger Cards of the Accounting;
b. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory-
taking of supplies and materials every six (6) months and prepare
duly certified Report on the Physical Count of Inventories for each
type of inventory and submit to the Office of the Auditor not later
than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively; and
c. Install an inventory management system for inventory items to
ensure proper monitoring and reporting.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Direct the Accountant and the Property Officer to continuously
exert effort in reconciling the variance on their respective records and
update the Property Cards (PCs) regularly;
b. Direct the Inventory Committee to submit a complete and reliable
Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment
(RPCPPE);
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to continuously exert efforts to
retrieve the necessary records and adequately identify the specific
transactions in the lumped amount covered by entries with
"unreconciled" notations and make the required adjustments to correct
the affected account balances;
d. Make a representation from the Sangguniang Bayan to obtain an
authority to formally transfer the properties, School Buildings, to the
respective users; and
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the necessary disclosure
required by the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
Henceforth, adherence to the reporting guidelines should faithfully
complied.

observations.recommendation: WerecommendedthattheManagementdirectthe LocalPovertyReductionActionTeam(LPRAT)to expedite the completion of the GPB projects identified to ensure efficient and effective completion thereof and undertake proper measures to revisit the plan of each project to introduce reinforcements or strategies in the revival of its operation and assist the beneficiaries for its sustainability.
observations.reason: This is still subject to continuous monitoring.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that Management:
a. Require the concerned Collecting Officer to remit his/her
collections intact and on time to the Municipal Liquidating
Officer; and
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to properly supervise the
collectors to observe timely remittance of collections to
minimize the risk of possible misappropriation.

2.5 We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to communicate and send written
notice to the payee on the existence of checks at least one month
before a check becomes stale; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust on the above-
mentioned stale checks.

3.11 We reiterate our recommendation that Management:
a. Direct the GSO to:
 practice the use of Request and Issue Slip (RIS) in every
issuance of inventory item to the end-users and submit the
monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI)
to MAO;
 maintain stock cards for inventories and conduct periodic
reconciliation of their records with the ledger cards of the
MAO;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to use the perpetual inventory
system in the recording of inventories; and
c. Augment personnel to GSO in order to enable them in executing
their mandated duties and functions.

4.9 We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that Management:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count
earlier to give enough time to prepare the RPCPPE, so any
discrepancy found in the accounting and property records may be
reconciled as soon as possible;
b. Require the Accountant and the GSO to exert efforts to compare
their respective records and verify/trace the unreconciled balances
and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct any
unrecorded/ erroneous recording of PPEs to come up with the
correct balances of the accounts; and
c. Augment personnel to GSO in order to enable them in executing
their mandated duties and functions.

5.6 We recommended that Management direct the GSO to:
a. Undertake immediate disposal of the subject properties to
prevent further deterioration and decline of value, in order for
the Municipality to generate additional income from sale thereof;
and
b. Conduct periodic disposal of the assets once it become
unserviceable and beyond economical repairs to avoid the
accumulation of unserviceable PPEs in the storage facility.

6.8 We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to prepare
adjusting journal entries to classify the above-mentioned equipment items
to its proper accounts in accordance with the description of accounts in
the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

7.5 We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant continue to exert efforts to retrieve the necessary records
and properly identify the specific transactions in the lumped amount
covered by entries with “for reconciliation and unreconciled” notations
and make the necessary adjustments to correct the affected account
balances.

8.7 We recommended that Management to:
a. implement the proper controls on the receipt and issuance of the
construction materials and submit to MAO the monthly Report
of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) for the construction of
projects by Administration; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the perpetual
inventory method of accounting for supplies and materials.

9.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation
of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to
identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts
and make necessary adjustments, if any.

WerecommendedthattheManagementdirectthe Municipal Treasurer to act as head of the treasury and do her responsibility to strictly safeguardthecashandensurepromptremittance oftheamountcollectedinordertoaccountwhat rightfullybelongstothegovernmentandinstitute thenecessaryappropriateaction,ifwarranted,by the circumstances.
observations.managementAction: The LCE orders the Municipal Treasurer to strictly implement the subject to timely remittance of collection to ensure proper safeguard and to charge appropriate action to erring collectors as necessary. As such, collectors were constantly reminded to remit collections intact and on time.
observations.reason: This is still subject to continuous monitoring and is reiterated in this report.

observations.managementAction: The LCE directs the MBO to reinforce PRDP I-BUILD Component Operations Manual with respect to creating of a separate trust fund account.
observations.reason: Separate Trust Fund account for the purpose was set up.

observations.recommendation: WerecommendedthattheManagementdirectthe Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) to intensify thoroughly the identification and validation of programs, projects, and activities to be in the LDRRMFIP and to stop the practice of charging items that are not related to disaster risk reduction and managementandadherestrictlytotheprovisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 in the utilization of LDRRMF of the Municipality.
observations.reason: This is still subject to continuous monitoring.

observations.recommendation: WerecommendedthattheManagementcausethe reorganization of the Municipal Solid Waste ManagementBoardtoconformwiththerequired memberssetbyRA9003anditsIRR.Also,direct the reorganized board to perform its functions and responsibilities to effectively implement the approved SWM Plan of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LCE positively responded to the restructuring of the Solid Waste Management Board and will employ qualified individuals that will carry its functions effectively.
observations.reason: A new set of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board was created. This is still subject to continuous monitoring.

observations.recommendation: 1.8		We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to act as head of the treasury to strictly safeguard the cash and ensure prompt remittance and deposit of the amount collected in order toaccount what rightfully belongs to the government and institute the necessary appropriate action, if warranted, by the circumstances.

3.13	We recommended that the Management
a.     direct the Inventory Committee to (i) exert extra effort to complete the physical inventory of all property, plant, and equipment and (ii) reconcile the report with the accounting records; and (iii) observe the consistent submission of the physical inventory reports on PPEs to the Office of the Auditor for review and validation not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to existing rules and regulations; and
b.     require the GSO Officer and Accountant to maintain property card (PC) and property plant and equipment ledger card (PPELC), respectively, and conduct a periodic reconciliation of the PPE accounts.

4.9		We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to retrieve the necessary records to properly classify the unidentified lump balances and to thoroughly trace and analyze the abnormal balances in the subsidiary ledgers and determine the reason for the erroneous entries in order to make the necessary adjustments to correct the same, in conformity with the provisions of COA Circular 2015-009 and the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGU.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor closely the remittances of the collecting officers as a deterrent to possible losses or any eventually that may lead to misstatements of Cash – Local Treasury and income accounts. Likewise, ensure prompt remittance of amount collected in order to account what rightfully belongs to the government and to institute the necessary appropriate action, if warranted, by the circumstances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office keep on reminding collectors for timely remittance of its cash collections on daily or weekly basis.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer directed her personnel to remit the collections intact and on time. Reiterated in Finding No.1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the inventory committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE and prepare correct format of RPCPPE that is PPE classified by type. Likewise, the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant should regularly reconcile the records so as to come up with the accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
observations.managementAction: A Memorandum Order No. 31 dated May 2, 2018 was issued to concern offices to appoint “Office Property Custodian” and conduct physical inventory of assets and submit to GSO for consolidation.
observations.reason: The appointed Office Property Custodians identified, classified, and counted properties in each office. However, Inventory Committee did not conduct comprehen-sive physical count thus reiterated in Finding No. 2.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality appropriate at least 1% of its IRA for the programs and activities to promote child care and welfare in accordance with Section 15 of RA 9344 known as the “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006”.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will provide 1% of the IRA for Local Council for the Protection of Children as mandated.
observations.reason: The agency has not yet appropriated 1% of its IRA for programs of Local Council for the Protection of Children.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 by submitting the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within the legal time frame.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive will remind the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant’s Office to have coordination in the submission of the said report.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO and MAccO were able to submit the said report during the 1st semester of this year but were not sustained throughout the year. Reiterated in Finding No. 5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management impose liquidated damages for the failure on the part of the supplier to completely deliver all items specified in the contract in accordance with Section 3 of Annex D of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The LGU received the funds from the Department of Agriculture in connection to this BUB Project more than 1 year from the date of its Award so the LGU opted not to deduct the liquidated damages from the supplier. Liquidated damages on the delayed delivery will be acted accordingly as required
observations.reason: MAccO was directed to strictly adhere to the rules and recommenda-tion and was directed to be watchful with the timeliness of deliveries of goods/services so that appropriate liquidated damages could be charged to the delinquent suppliers if any.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) do their duties and responsibilities especially in adopting revenue-generating measures to beat-up budget constraints of the Municipality in order to promote and ensure effective and efficient implementation of solid waste management projects and programs.
observations.managementAction: Regular conduct of information and education campaign for the ecological solid waste management has been done among schools and during barangay assembly meetings. Programs and projects were lobbied to national agencies for funding.
observations.reason: The agency committed to adhere with the recommenda-tions. Reiterated in Finding No. 13.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) perform its responsibility and revisit plan of each project and introduce improvements to revive its operation and institutionalize monitoring and evaluation system to evaluate its sustainability. Also, they should effectively and efficiently identify priority poverty reduction projects to help the Municipality attain its mission of improving the quality life of its people.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed the BUB Focal Person to review and monitor the projects implemented in relation to the local poverty reduction of the LGU.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 10.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Committee optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning, effective and efficient implementation and strict evaluation and monitoring to ensure accomplishment of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive called a meeting with the Municipal Development Council to tackle issues on delayed project implementation which resulted to under-utilization of the development fund. The MPDC is tasked to strictly conduct evaluation and monitoring to ensure timely implementation of the projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to act as head of the treasury and do her responsibility to strictly safeguard the cash and ensure prompt remittance of the amount collected in order to account what rightfully belongs to the government and institute the necessary appropriate action, if warranted, by the circumstances.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all PPE and to the MGSO and the MAO to give priority in maintaining property and ledger cards to aid in their regular reconciliation to come up with the accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
We recommended that the Management direct the office in charge of filing and paying tax returns to comply with the regulations set forth in BIR RR No. 5-2015.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to install a scheme to facilitate the timely remittance of withheld amount so as not to deprive the officials and employees of further benefits and to avoid the burden of any surcharges and interests not due to them.

We recommended that the Management require the LDRRMO to regularly submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization duly prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor so that any deficiency noted therein is promptly communicated to the management.

We recommended that the Management employs means to ensure that all contracts are submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from the perfection thereof and require the personnel in-charge to submit the delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance pursuant to the above-mentioned regulations.

We recommended that the management strictly comply with the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to the prescribed minimum net take home pay of the employees to promote effective and efficient public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support their daily needs until the next payday.

We recommended that the Municipal Project Management and Implementation Unit (MPMIU) revisit the Implementation Management Agreement (IMA) and cause the Municipality’s compliance with the post-implementation activities of the project. Also, they should monitor the proper operation and maintenance of the project for its sustainability and efficient use.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and require the Municipal Accountant to verify and analyze the negative balances noted in the Advances to Officers and Employees account to effect proper and appropriate procedures for these abnormal balances.
observations.managementAction: Unliquidated cash advances of employees were directly deducted to payrolls. Demand letters and tracers were prepared and sent to accountable officers. Accounts which are no longer connected to LGU were transferred to Other Receivables.
observations.reason: Part of the outstanding balance was the CIF the former Mayor, of which liquidation were already sent to ICFAU, waiting for its corresponding credit notice.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the officers concerned to monitor the implementation of the projects relative to the funds transferred. Likewise, continue sending demand letters to the implementing agencies for immediate liquidation and demand return of the funds if found unutilized or not used, otherwise, no succeeding funds shall be released to the same implementing agencies in compliance with COA Circular 94-013.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to PO’s and Other Local Government Units.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accounting Office had exerted effort in requiring POs and Other LGUs to liquidate their outstanding balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions and to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Reciprocal accounts will be reviewed and proper adjustments will be prepared.
observations.reason: Adjustments are yet to be made for reconciliation is still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging items that are not related to disaster risk reduction and management and adhere strictly to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Annual Investment Plan and Annual Procurement Plan were properly reviewed in order to justify programs, projects and activities in compliance to the guidelines before disbursements.
observations.reason: MDRRMO and MAccO were directed to strictly adhere to the rules and recommenda-tion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Requisitioning Officer to ensure and certify that the drugs and medicines being procured are listed in the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF), Volume I, current edition in compliance with Paragraph II.2 of DOH Administrative Order No. 163.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive reminds the Municipal Health Officer to ensure that all drugs and medicines procured will be in compliance and listed in the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF).
observations.reason: The Municipal Health Officer issued a certification that all drugs and medicines purchased were listed in the Philippine National Drug Formulary.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the concerned office to properly and completely accomplish the Purchase Orders indicating all the vital information as guide to the suppliers in complying the terms and conditions of the contract as provided in COA Circular No. 96-010. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to be vigilant in enforcing liquidating damages for the late or non-deliveries of procured goods and services.
Moreover, Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the requirements on the submission of the perfected contracts and purchase orders irrespective of the amount and the supporting documents, within five (5) working days pursuant to the afore-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive immediately called a meeting with the BAC to properly and completely accomplish the PO’s indicating all the needed information to guide the suppliers in complying the terms and conditions of the contract strictly observing the dates of awards and dated of delivery of items .
observations.reason: BAC was directed to strictly adhere to the rules and recommenda-tion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all expenses are properly taken up in the accounts of the year they were incurred and refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures to current year’s appropriation in accordance with Section 4 of PD 1445 and Section 350 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive orders the Municipal Budget and Accountant Office to estimate expenses already incurred for appropriation and to be included in the current year’s expenditures.
observations.reason: The agency committed to adhere with the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor closely the remittances of the collecting officers as a deterrent to possible losses or any eventually that may lead to misstatements of Cash – Local Treasury and income accounts. Likewise, ensure prompt remittance of amount collected in order to account what rightfully belongs to the government and to institute the necessary appropriate action, if warranted, by the circumstances.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and require the Municipal Accountant to verify and analyze the negative balances noted in the Advances to Officers and Employees account to effect proper and appropriate procedures for these abnormal balances.

We recommended that the Management require the officers concerned to monitor the implementation of the projects relative to the funds transferred. Likewise, continue sending demand letters to the implementing agencies for immediate liquidation and demand return of the funds if found unutilized or not used, otherwise, no succeeding funds shall be released to the same implementing agencies in compliance with COA Circular 94-013.

We recommended that the Management instruct the inventory committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE and prepare correct format of RPCPPE that is PPE classified by type. Likewise, the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant should regularly reconcile the records so as to come up with the accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions and to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipality appropriate at least 1% of its IRA for the programs and activities to promote child care and welfare in accordance with Section 15 of RA 9344 known as the “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006”.
We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 by submitting the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within the legal time frame.

We recommended that management stop the practice of charging items that are not related to disaster risk reduction and management and adhere strictly to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management impose liquidated damages for the failure on the part of the supplier to completely deliver all items specified in the contract in accordance with Section 3 of Annex D of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management require the Requisitioning Officer to ensure and certify that the drugs and medicines being procured are listed in the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF), Volume I, current edition in compliance with Paragraph II.2 of DOH Administrative Order No. 163.

We recommended that the Management require the concerned office to properly and completely accomplish the Purchase Orders indicating all the vital information as guide to the suppliers in complying the terms and conditions of the contract as provided in COA Circular No. 96-010. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to be vigilant in enforcing liquidating damages for the late or non-deliveries of procured goods and services.
Moreover, Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the requirements on the submission of the perfected contracts and purchase orders irrespective of the amount and the supporting documents, within five (5) working days pursuant to the afore-cited regulations.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all expenses are properly taken up in the accounts of the year they were incurred and refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures to current year’s appropriation in accordance with Section 4 of PD 1445 and Section 350 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) do their duties and responsibilities especially in adopting revenue-generating measures to beat-up budget constraints of the Municipality in order to promote and ensure effective and efficient implementation of solid waste management projects and programs.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to Section 26 of COA Circular 92-382 and direct the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor the daily remittances of collections of all Revenue Collectors so as not to expose cash to possible loss and/or misappropriation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury Office conducted a meeting and called the attention of all personnel handling cash collections and they were reminded of the said guidelines. The concerned personnel, the revenue collectors agreed and promised to remit their collections intact and on time.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer directed her personnel to remit the collections intact and on time. Reiterated in Finding No.1.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS regularly and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor and to appropriately effect necessary treatment of the reconciling items pursuant to Section Nos.  3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has made the effort in securing Bank Statements in order to prepare and submit the BRS on time.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accoun-tant has made the effort in preparing and submitting the BRS on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and compel all officers and employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances and advice the Municipal Accountant to stop the granting of additional cash advances unless all outstanding cash advances have been liquidated.
observations.managementAction: Closely monitored the concerned employees.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 2.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to fast track the conduct of the physical count of all the fixed assets and submit the Report of the Physical Inventory of PPE to Commission on Audit pursuant to Sec. 124 of the NGAs manual to establish the correct value of its assets.
observations.managementAction: Closely monitored the concerned committee.
observations.reason: Inventory and Manage-ment Committee has not yet started the physical inventory

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management create a General Services Office spearheaded by the designated General Services Officer with sufficient support from the top management to gain the cooperation or confidence of the auditee and to strengthen the implementation of supply and property management and other related general services to enable the agency to better respond to the requirement of the public they serve.
observations.managementAction: The agency has designated already someone to act as GSO for the meantime while the plan to create GSO is still on process.
observations.reason: A Municipal GSO-Designate has been designated.

observations.recommendation: Barangay Shares from real property taxes, community taxes and other service income should be remitted/released to the different barangays within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 271 (d) of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 so as not to impede the implementation of the programmed plans and projects for its constituents.
observations.managementAction: As of 2017, the Barangay shares have been released per quarter already.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the employee or official concerned to submit vouchers with the supporting documents within the prescribed period of submission for timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy and to point out to management whatever defects found thereon for corrective measures.
observations.managementAction: MAO and MTO coordinated in order to fully implement the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accoun-tant and Municipal Treasurer has made the effort in submitting transaction documents on time.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee and the General Services Office to accomplish properly the purchase orders and should see to it that the required conditions are indicated therein. These purchase orders shall then be submitted to the Office of the Auditor irrespective of the amount within five (5) working days pursuant to the abovementioned regulations. 
Likewise, they shall submit copies of inspection of deliveries of goods and services within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to enable this office to conduct timely review and validation.
observations.managementAction: BAC was directed to strictly adhere to the rules and recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11.

observations.managementAction: BAC was directed to strictly adhere to the rules and recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management develop and implement enforcement technique to ensure viability and effective implementation of solid waste management, to enhance awareness on existing laws, rules and regulations and strictly observed the provisions thereof.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO is now doing what has been recommended in the COA AOM vis a vis, waste segregation in every Barangay, TF Kahinlo has been implemented, as well as the issuance of citation tickets to the violators. Dumping is no longer practiced.
observations.reason: MENRO has formed TF Kahinlo to facilitate the implementation of proper segrega-tion

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management always secure authority thru Sangunian Bayan (SB) Resolution for the disbursements of appropriations specified in generic terms. Also, we recommend that the priority projects should be specifically identified in the Annual Investment Plan for proper implementation and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The agency committed to adhere with the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU ensure that the utilization of LDRRMF should be in accordance with the existing laws, rules and regulations and that all procurement should be in compliance with the revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The agency committed to adhere with the recommenda-tions.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board fully utilize the budget allocated for the programs and projects under the SEF, shall faithfully adhere to the provisions of the Local Government Code (RA 7160) and the Joint Circular of DECS, DBM and DILG and ensure that Special Education Fund will be appropriated and utilized exclusively for the purpose, with due considerations of the priority needs and the benefits for the students as a whole.

Direct the concerned personnel in the project implementation to speed up implementation and completion of the projects. The Municipal officials should also adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular no. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM in the appropriation and utilization of 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The agency committed to adhere with the recommenda-tions.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 15.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the institutionalization of GAD Database or Sex-Disaggregated Data as prescribed under Section 4.4 of the PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 for effective and efficient identification of priority gender issued to be addressed with the GAD Plan and Budget and the management to direct the concerned officer to prepare and submit the reports on time.
observations.managementAction: The agency committed to adhere with the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: Direct the concerned office to take yearly physical count of its assets and state in the inventory report the actual condition of the said assets, whether serviceable or not, so as to make proper adjustments and reconciliations in the books.
observations.managementAction: The different offices initially conducted inventory of its office equipment under their respective accountability however was not sustained due to the absence GSO who has a vital part in the conduct of the said inventory taking.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to correct the above-stated deficient practices and impose disciplinary action on the concerned collecting officer.  Likewise, the municipal treasurer should take custody of the accountable forms and monitor closely the remittance of collections at least once a week.
observations.managementAction: Revenue collectors were directed to remit their collections to the Liquidating Officer every day.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to Section 26 of COA Circular 92-382 and direct the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor the daily remittances of collections of all Revenue Collectors so as not to expose cash to possible loss and/or misappropriation.

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS regularly and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor and to appropriately effect necessary treatment of the reconciling items pursuant to Section Nos.  3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and compel all officers and employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances and advice the Municipal Accountant to stop the granting of additional cash advances unless all outstanding cash advances have been liquidated.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to fast track the conduct of the physical count of all the fixed assets and submit the Report of the Physical Inventory of PPE to Commission on Audit pursuant to Sec. 124 of the NGAs manual to establish the correct value of its assets.

We recommend that Management create a General Services Office spearheaded by the designated General Services Officer with sufficient support from the top management to gain the cooperation or confidence of the auditee and to strengthen the implementation of supply and property management and other related general services to enable the agency to better respond to the requirement of the public they serve.

Barangay Shares from real property taxes, community taxes and other service income should be remitted/released to the different barangays within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 271 (d) of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 so as not to impede the implementation of the programmed plans and projects for its constituents.

Instruct the employee or official concerned to submit vouchers with the supporting documents within the prescribed period of submission for timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy and to point out to management whatever defects found thereon for corrective measures.

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee and the General Services Office to accomplish properly the purchase orders and should see to it that the required conditions are indicated therein. These purchase orders shall then be submitted to the Office of the Auditor irrespective of the amount within five (5) working days pursuant to the abovementioned regulations.

Likewise, they shall submit copies of inspection of deliveries of goods and services within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to enable this office to conduct timely review and validation.

We recommend that management develop and implement enforcement technique to ensure viability and effective implementation of solid waste management, to enhance awareness on existing laws, rules and regulations and strictly observed the provisions thereof.

We recommend that management always secure authority thru Sangunian Bayan (SB) Resolution for the disbursements of appropriations specified in generic terms. Also, we recommend that the priority projects should be specifically identified in the Annual Investment Plan for proper implementation and monitoring.

We recommend that the LGU ensure that the utilization of LDRRMF should be in accordance with the existing laws, rules and regulations and that all procurement should be in compliance with the revised IRR of RA 9184.

Direct the concerned officers to exercise prudence in the use of limited cash resources and make certain that Cash Precautionary Reserves is strictly maintained and monitored. Further, management should ensure that corresponding cash is available before entering into contracts or approving purchase orders.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant closed the trust liability accounts of the projects/ programs fully liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to impose stricter control in the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
All long outstanding cash advances shall be issued with an order of payment, and failure to liquidate the said cash advances shall be a valid ground for the withholding of his/her salary and other sanctions, as warranted.
observations.managementAction: Agreements were made with the accountable officers relative to their advances which will be deducted from their payroll and other claims due them. Also, they committed to strengthen its efforts in reducing the unliquidated cash advance.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances to elective officials and to appointed officials and employees not designated as Special Disbursing Officer for purposes other than for their official traveling expenses, in conformity with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LCE has designated a Special Disbursing Officer.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin Management to strictly adhere with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations on all government procurement. Stop the practice of granting cash advances for purchase of goods and services and on account of the agency’s programs and activities. Lastly, ensure that all payments must be made by check except when the circumstances call for impracticability, difficulty and impossibility of payments through checks.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had already stopped the granting of cash advances for the procurement of equipment and supplies since June 2015, except for expenses related to special programs and activities like the Naliyagan and Pangadu Festival granted to Special disbursing Officer/LCE.

observations.recommendation: Compliance of the Joint Circular mentioned above is strictly enjoined so as not to affect the implementation of other programmed projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management will strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting and liquidation of the Confidential Fund.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin Management to exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds which shall be used solely for general public purposes as provided for under Section 4.2 of P.D. 1445. Financial assistance to private or individual person shall be coursed through the municipal social welfare office subject to the established guidelines and the required case study conducted by that office.
observations.managementAction: MSWD Office has adopted the DSWD guidelines in granting of Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation (AICS).

observations.recommendation: Enjoin the Local School Board should faithfully adhere to the provisions of the Local Code (RA 7160) and the Joint Circular of DECS, DBM and DILG and ensure that Special Education Fund will be utilized exclusively for the purpose for which it was created to prevent disallowance in the future.
observations.managementAction: Local School Board will faithfully adhere to the provisions.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive, being the primary responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to his agency, should direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit the required financial and other reports and supporting documents in such form and within the period prescribed by the Commission.
observations.managementAction: Management made certain that close coordination between the Accounting and Treasury Office will be strengthened to ensure the timely submission of accounts and other required reports to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Engineering Office to strictly monitor the implementation so projects will be finished as planned and to observe the regulation in furnishing the Office of the Auditor of certified copies of the suspension orders for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension are commendable or not specifically for projects under contract.
observations.managementAction: Directed the Engineering Office to strictly monitor the implementation of the projects so that it will be finished as planned.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin Management to take the necessary safety nets relative to availing credit facilities to preclude occurrence of needing to allocate a lion’s share of their Internal Revenue Allotment to repay loans leaving little funds for the delivery of essential public services in the future.
observations.managementAction: LGU, before availing of any additional loans to credit institutions, will review its capacity to pay and will consider fund availability in order not to hamper other priority programs, projects and activities which are necessary in the LGU’s operation.
